corpus.title: World Cup? Not today: New York is celebrating the Knicks
corpus.signal_text:
World Cup? Not today: New York is celebrating the Knicks

The FIFA World Cup may be under way in the United States, but in New York on Thursday, all eyes were on basketball. Tens of thousands of fans packed the streets of Manhattan to celebrate the NBA champion Knicks as the team paraded through the city under tight security. FRANCE 24’s Juliette Montilly was there.

corpus.title: New York: des coups de feu tirés à Times Square, sans faire de blessé selon la police
corpus.signal_text:
New York: des coups de feu tirés à Times Square, sans faire de blessé selon la police

Selon les autorités, aucun blessé n'est à déplorer et aucune interpellation n'a eu lieu à ce stade. Les investigations se poursuivent afin de déterminer les circonstances exactes de l'incident.

Mamdani’s Office Makes Embarrassing Mistake For Knicks Championship Parade

The New York Knicks celebrated their long-awaited NBA Finals win with a parade on Thursday, and fans couldn’t help but notice a glaring mistake at City Hall. Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s administration hung banners for each player on the team, showcasing their last names and jersey numbers — and fans quickly noticed that something was amiss. ...

corpus.title: Shooting In New York's Times Square Trigger Panic; One Injured, Juvenile Suspect Held
corpus.signal_text:
Shooting In New York's Times Square Trigger Panic; One Injured, Juvenile Suspect Held

Gunfire in New York City's Times Square caused panic as tourists and commuters fled for safety. A juvenile suspect has been arrested, and one person was injured, with the degree of injuries undisclosed. The shooting, occurring near West 44th Street and Seventh Avenue on Thursday afternoon, is believed to have stemmed from a dispute, not a random attack.

corpus.title: Smashed vehicles, littered streets in NYC as hundreds of fans erupt in chaotic celebration of Knicks' first NBA title in 53 years
corpus.signal_text:
Smashed vehicles, littered streets in NYC as hundreds of fans erupt in chaotic celebration of Knicks' first NBA title in 53 years

New York City descended into disorder over the weekend as thousands of Knicks fans flooded the streets following the team’s 94-90 victory over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, securing their first championship in 53 years.
